summary: CC-0013 implements the KeystoneReconciler with 6 sequential sub-reconcilers (secrets, fernetKeys, config, database, deployment, bootstrap), each gating the next via status conditions. All previous review feedback from greptile-apps[bot] has been addressed: ESO Secret watch uses EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc instead of EnqueueRequestForOwner, resource names are instance-scoped via CR name, reconcile ordering matches data dependencies (config before database/bootstrap), and Jobs mount the config ConfigMap. The implementation is thorough, well-tested (60+ test cases), follows existing patterns, and handles edge cases. All verification checks pass: go test, go vet, golangci-lint, gosec.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./operators/keystone/... — 0 failures)
Edge cases covered (missing secrets, stale jobs, brownfield mode, brownfield skips MariaDB CRs, min key floor)
Error cases tested (API errors, job failures, ErrJobFailed sentinel)
Condition messages and ObservedGeneration verified in assertions
Sequential progression tested (phase 1 → phase 2 with added prerequisites)
Idempotency tested (two reconciles produce same result)
Secret watch mapper tested for referenced, owned, and unrelated secrets

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing codebase patterns (gomega, conditions, Ensure* shared libraries)
One file per sub-reconciler, clear separation of concerns
Error wrapping with context in all error paths
Functions are focused and reasonably sized
No dead code or unused imports

security_checklist.item: DB password handled via url.UserPassword (RFC 3986 userinfo encoding)
Fernet rotation CronJob uses minimal RBAC (get+patch on specific Secret)
Rotation script uses TLS with proper CA verification

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is appropriately complex for the requirements
Reconcile ordering matches data dependencies (config before db_sync and bootstrap)
Secret watch correctly uses MapFunc instead of EnqueueRequestForOwner for ESO secrets
All child resource names are instance-scoped (use CR name prefix)
Owner references set on all created resources
main.go registers external schemes and instantiates reconciler correctly

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated logic across sub-reconcilers (each has distinct responsibility)
Shared libraries reused (conditions, config, database, deployment, job, secrets, plugins, policy)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Sub-reconcilers return early on not-ready conditions
Stale job detection prevents silently skipping migrations

defensive_checklist.item: ExternalSecret and Secret readiness both verified (status-vs-object race guard)
MaxActiveKeys floor of 3 enforced in code (defense-in-depth alongside CRD marker)
Credential-keys volume marked Optional to prevent failures until future feature
NotFound errors handled gracefully in Reconcile()

issues_found.description: REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comment style is not production-appropriate. The comment at reconcile_config.go:81-83 documents a valid technical decision (url.UserPassword vs url.PathEscape) but uses a reviewer-debate format instead of a standard explanatory comment.
issues_found.fix: Rewrite as: // url.UserPassword correctly encodes all RFC 3986 userinfo characters (including @, :, /). // url.PathEscape does not escape @ or :, which would produce an invalid connection URL.

issues_found.description: TODO comment for credential key management is flagged by stub scanner but is an explicitly documented deferral per the plan's exclusion section. The volume is marked Optional=true to prevent failures.
issues_found.fix: No action required — this is an intentional deferral with graceful fallback. The TODO tracks future work.

suggested_improvements: Consider rewriting the REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comment at reconcile_config.go:81 as a standard technical rationale comment
Consider extracting the per-test-file scheme helpers (secretsTestScheme, dbTestScheme, etc.) into a shared testutil if more sub-reconciler test files are added in the future
next_steps: CC-0014: Integration/E2E tests for the Keystone reconciler
Future: Credential key management sub-reconciler (reconcileCredentialKeys) to make the credential-keys volume non-optional
Future: Federation support in reconcileConfig (FederationSpec exists but no reconciler logic)
